questions.prompt: A customer's site is hammering the server. The MSP wants to stop it immediately but investigate before killing anything. Which command fits?
choices: kill -9 every process owned by the account user
questions.explanation: cgroup:freeze pauses every process in the account's cgroup without killing them. State is preserved; you can investigate, then kill the specific offender, then thaw the rest. The other options either kill state or take longer effect.

questions.prompt: Disk quota in ApisCP is per cgroup (per account), not per Unix user.
questions.explanation: Disk quota is per Unix user (account user, with a separately tracked apache-user tally that ApisCP aggregates). cgroups bound CPU, memory, and I/O; storage quota is a different kernel mechanism.
